content:
🌟 cursor-clip | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a visually intuitive and dynamically positioned clipboard history manager on Wayland compositors, enhancing productivity and clipboard management.
✨ Key Features: • Windows 11-style clipboard history • Dynamic positioning at cursor location • Native GNOME/Libadwaita design • Advanced Wayland integration (Layer Shell Protocol, Cursor Tracking) • Multi-output and Multi-input support
📖 Summary: Cursor Clip is a modern Wayland clipboard manager built with Rust and GTK4, offering a Windows 11-style clipboard history that dynamically positions itself at the mouse cursor. It integrates seamlessly with the GNOME design language and Wayland protocols for reliable clipboard handling.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
